name: Socket.dev
pricing_notes: Free: public repos; Team $25/dev/mo; Enterprise custom. ~$65M raised.

performance.notes: Deep package analysis adds latency; results cached for known packages

agent_readiness.notes: Supply chain security for npm/PyPI/Go packages. Detects malware, typosquatting, protestware, compromised maintainers. GitHub integration blocks PRs introducing risky deps. Proactive (behavior-based) vs reactive (CVE-based).

gotchas: Proactive detection can produce false positives requiring review
Primarily npm-focused; Python/Go support less mature
Block-on-PR workflow needs team buy-in to avoid friction
Pricing jumps steeply for team plans
best_when: Supply chain security is a priority (post-SolarWinds/XZ world)
Agent pipelines that install or recommend npm/Python packages
Teams wanting proactive malware detection vs just CVE scanning
CI/CD pipelines that need to gate on dependency risk
avoid_when: CVE-only security requirements (Snyk is more mature)
Teams that can't handle false positive workflow friction
summary: Socket.dev provides proactive supply chain security for npm and Python packages, detecting malware, typosquatting, and compromised maintainers before they enter your codebase. Behavior-based analysis (not just CVE lookups) catches novel threats. Used by major companies post-SolarWinds. Particularly relevant for agents that make package recommendations or install dependencies.
